behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real Git rename detection plus CLI/report wiring, not just a local classifier function. A mock of diff/status output can hide real cases where Git emits rename metadata differently (or not at all), causing double-counting. Real subprocess + real repo state is needed. Higher levels are disqualified because the behavior has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows exactly one rename and zero paired add/delete entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real Git binary-diff behavior and subprocess/stdout handling. In-process fakes often return sanitized text and miss decode/crash paths triggered by actual binary diff output. No AI/ML output quality is involved, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a real binary file change (e.g., image bytes), run the CLI subprocess, verify exit code is success and binary file contributes to counts without traceback/crash.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is handling real Git errors/exit codes for unborn HEAD in an actual repository. Mocking command responses can hide incorrect subprocess error handling and shell invocation behavior. No model decision-making or agent chain exists, so higher AI levels are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess in that repo, and assert it returns a valid empty report and non-error exit status.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim over real commit history semantics. It crosses module boundaries and depends on real Git history traversal and CLI parsing/orchestration; fakes can mask mismatches between expected and actual Git output. No AI/ML dependency or multi-agent pipeline is present.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across multiple commits, run the CLI subprocess, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from the scripted history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This relies on real Git index vs working-tree behavior, which is OS+Git stateful infrastructure. Mocking index/worktree states tends to hide real staging semantics and command differences. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no LLM/model decisions are part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged (`git add`), run the CLI subprocess for each state, and assert normalized outputs are identical.
